global_config.visual_style: Cinematic, realistic, bright, natural lighting, modern aesthetic.
global_config.setting_summary_cn: 一个现代大学生的宿舍，以及一个宽敞明亮、人来人往的大型购物中心。
global_config.setting_summary_en: A modern college student's dorm room, and a spacious, bright, and bustling shopping mall.

character_definitions.name: 张天明
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A male Chinese college student in his early 20s, with a fashionable but slightly troubled expression. He is wearing casual clothes.

character_definitions.name: 柯林
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A friendly and helpful male Chinese college student in his early 20s, energetic and proactive.

character_definitions.name: 林雪梅
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A female Chinese college student in her early 20s, cheerful and observant.

character_definitions.name: 丽莎
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A female Chinese college student in her early 20s, stylish and opinionated.

hanzi: 张天明从家里来的时候,妈妈给他买了一些衣服,像T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤什么的,可是他觉得无论是样子还是颜色都不太好。
pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng cóng jiā lǐ lái de shí hou mā ma gěi tā mǎi le yì xiē yī fu xiàng T xù shān máo yī niú zǎi kù shén me de kě shì tā jué de wú lùn shì yàng zi hái shi yán sè dōu bú tài hǎo
translation: When Zhang Tianming came from home, his mom had bought him some clothes, like T-shirts, sweaters, jeans, and so on, but he felt that neither the style nor the color was very good.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示张天明对妈妈买的衣服不满意，引出购物的动机。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 在张天明的宿舍里，他从一个行李箱里拿出几件衣服（T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤），皱着眉头看着它们，显得很苦恼。衣服的款式和颜色看起来有些过时。
scenes.setting_en: A typical college dorm room, slightly messy. A suitcase is open on the bed.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic medium shot, slow zoom in. A young Chinese man, Zhang Tianming, in his dorm room, looking frustrated. He holds up an unfashionable T-shirt and a dull-colored sweater from a suitcase on his bed. The clothes look like something a mom would buy. Natural light from a window.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 张天明从家里来的时候,妈妈给他买了一些衣服,像T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤什么的,可是他觉得无论是样子还是颜色都不太好。
voice_over.pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng cóng jiā lǐ lái de shí hou mā ma gěi tā mǎi le yì xiē yī fu xiàng T xù shān máo yī niú zǎi kù shén me de kě shì tā jué de wú lùn shì yàng zi hái shi yán sè dōu bú tài hǎo
voice_over.translation: When Zhang Tianming came from home, his mom had bought him some clothes, like T-shirts, sweaters, jeans, and so on, but he felt that neither the style nor the color was very good.

hanzi: 今天是星期日,正好林雪梅和丽莎也需要买卫生纸、牙膏、毛巾、洗衣粉这些日用品,柯林就带他们来到附近一家最大的购物中心。
pinyin: jīn tiān shì xīng qī rì zhèng hǎo Lín Xuěméi hé Lìshā yě xū yào mǎi wèi shēng zhǐ yá gāo máo jīn xǐ yī fěn zhè xiē rì yòng pǐn Kē Lín jiù dài tā men lái dào fù jìn yì jiā zuì dà de gòu wù zhōng xīn
translation: Today is Sunday. It just so happens that Lin Xuemei and Lisha also need to buy daily necessities like toilet paper, toothpaste, towels, and laundry detergent, so Ke Lin took them to the biggest shopping center nearby.

scenes.scene_goal: 交代故事发生的时间和地点，展示四个朋友一起去购物中心。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 星期天，阳光明媚。张天明、柯林、林雪梅和丽莎四人正一起走向一个大型现代化购物中心的入口。他们有说有笑，气氛轻松愉快。
scenes.setting_en: The exterior of a large, modern shopping mall on a sunny day.
scenes.camera_movement: Pan right to follow the group
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic wide shot, panning right. Four young Chinese college students (two male, two female) are walking cheerfully towards the entrance of a huge, modern shopping mall. It's a bright, sunny Sunday. The mall has glass walls and prominent logos. Other shoppers are walking around.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 今天是星期日,正好林雪梅和丽莎也需要买日用品,柯林就带他们来到附近一家最大的购物中心。
voice_over.pinyin: jīn tiān shì xīng qī rì zhèng hǎo Lín Xuěméi hé Lìshā yě xū yào mǎi rì yòng pǐn Kē Lín jiù dài tā men lái dào fù jìn yì jiā zuì dà de gòu wù zhōng xīn
voice_over.translation: Today is Sunday. It just so happens that Lin Xuemei and Lisha also need to buy daily necessities, so Ke Lin took them to the biggest shopping center nearby.

pinyin: nǐ yào mǎi shén me yī fu
translation: What clothes do you want to buy?

scenes.scene_goal: 开启关于买衣服的具体对话。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 在购物中心的一家运动服装店里，柯林转头问身边的张天明。背景是挂满各种运动服的货架。
scenes.setting_en: Inside a bright, modern sportswear store within the shopping mall. Racks of colorful athletic apparel are visible.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic medium two-shot. Inside a sportswear store. Ke Lin turns to his friend Zhang Tianming and asks a question with a friendly expression. Racks of tracksuits and athletic wear are in the background.

voice_over.speaker: 柯林
voice_over.pinyin: nǐ yào mǎi shén me yī fu
voice_over.translation: What clothes do you want to buy?

pinyin: wǒ xiǎng mǎi yí tào yùndòngfú
translation: I'd like to buy a tracksuit.

scenes.scene_goal: 明确张天明的购物目标：运动服。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头切换到张天明，他一边浏览着周围的衣服，一边回答柯林的问题。
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic medium close-up on Zhang Tianming. He looks around the store thoughtfully before answering his friend. He seems determined to find something he likes.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.pinyin: wǒ xiǎng mǎi yí tào yùndòngfú
voice_over.translation: I'd like to buy a tracksuit.

hanzi: 这边儿就是。你看看这一套,样子、大小、长短都合适,而且打八折。
pinyin: zhèbiānr jiùshì nǐ kànkan zhè yí tào yàngzi dàxiǎo chángduǎn dōu héshì érqiě dǎ bāzhé
translation: They're right over here. Take a look at this set. The style, size, and length are all perfect, and it's 20% off.

scenes.scene_goal: 柯林为张天明推荐了一套具体的运动服。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 柯林兴奋地指向旁边货架上的一套运动服，并伸手把它拿下来展示给张天明看。镜头可以给运动服和上面的“八折”标签一个特写。
scenes.setting_en: Inside the sportswear store, near a clothing rack.
scenes.camera_movement: Starts on Ke Lin, then quick cut to a close-up of the price tag
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot, with a close-up insert
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic medium shot. Ke Lin enthusiastically points to a tracksuit on a rack, takes it off the hanger to show Zhang Tianming. Quick insert shot of a red price tag showing '八折' (20% off). Ke Lin gestures to the tracksuit, explaining its good points.

voice_over.speaker: 柯林
voice_over.hanzi: 这边儿就是。你看看这一套,样子、大小、长短都合适,而且打八折。
voice_over.pinyin: zhèbiānr jiùshì nǐ kànkan zhè yí tào yàngzi dàxiǎo chángduǎn dōu héshì érqiě dǎ bāzhé
voice_over.translation: They're right over here. Take a look at this set. The style, size, and length are all perfect, and it's 20% off.

hanzi: 哎, 柯林, 你身上这件衣服怎么是名牌的,你不是不穿名牌的吗?
我说不一定非买名牌的不可,没说过不穿名牌呀。这件是打折的时候买的。
pinyin: āi Kē Lín nǐ shēnshang zhè jiàn yīfu zěnme shì míngpái de nǐ búshì bù chuān míngpái de ma
wǒ shuō bù yídìng fēi mǎi míngpái de bùkě méi shuō guo bù chuān míngpái ya zhè jiàn shì dǎzhé de shíhou mǎi de
translation: Hey, Ke Lin, how come the shirt you're wearing is a name brand? Don't you not wear name brands?
I said you don't necessarily have to buy name brands, I never said I don't wear them. I bought this one when it was on sale.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过朋友间的调侃，引出关于“名牌”和“打折”的消费观念。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 服装区，朋友们正在聊天。丽莎突然注意到柯林身上的衣服，她笑着指了指，好奇地发问。柯林先是有点不好意思，然后立刻理直气壮地解释，还指了指衣服，表示是打折买的。
scenes.setting_en: In a brightly lit, modern clothing section of a department store. Racks of clothes are visible in the background.
scenes.camera_movement: The camera focuses on Lisha as she speaks, then quickly pans to Ke Lin for his reaction and reply.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic medium shot of four friends in a clothing store. Lisha, a stylish young woman, playfully points at her friend Ke Lin's shirt, a teasing smile on her face. Ke Lin looks slightly embarrassed, then smiles and gestures to his shirt as he explains himself. The other two friends watch with amusement.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 哎, 柯林, 你身上这件衣服怎么是名牌的,你不是不穿名牌的吗?
voice_over.pinyin: āi Kē Lín nǐ shēnshang zhè jiàn yīfu zěnme shì míngpái de nǐ búshì bù chuān míngpái de ma
voice_over.translation: Hey, Ke Lin, how come the shirt you're wearing is a name brand? Don't you not wear name brands?

hanzi: 哎,丽莎,我们去日用品那边看看。
你们去吧,我先去付钱,我们一会儿见。
pinyin: āi Lìshā wǒmen qù rìyòngpǐn nàbiān kànkan
nǐmen qù ba wǒ xiān qù fùqián wǒmen yíhuìr jiàn
translation: Hey, Lisha, let's go check out the daily necessities section.
You guys go ahead. I'm going to pay first. I'll see you in a bit.

scenes.scene_goal: 表现人物购物路线的分歧，并推动剧情转向下一个场景——付钱。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 讨论告一段落，张天明想去别处逛逛，他转向丽莎发出邀请，并用手指了指远处“日用品”的牌子。丽莎摇摇头，拿着自己选好的衣服，示意她要去收银台，并向他们挥手告别。
scenes.setting_en: Still in the clothing section, near an aisle with signs pointing to 'Daily Necessities' and 'Checkout'.
scenes.camera_movement: The camera follows Zhang Tianming as he turns to Lisha and gestures, then focuses on Lisha as she replies and waves.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Wide shot of the four friends. Zhang Tianming suggests going to another section, pointing off-screen. Lisha shakes her head with a smile, holds up some clothes, and points towards the checkout counter before waving them off.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 哎,丽莎,我们去日用品那边看看。
voice_over.pinyin: āi Lìshā wǒmen qù rìyòngpǐn nàbiān kànkan
voice_over.translation: Hey, Lisha, let's go check out the daily necessities section.

hanzi: 先生,付现金,还是用信用卡?
pinyin: xiānsheng fù xiànjīn háishi yòng xìnyòngkǎ
translation: Sir, will you be paying with cash or credit card?

scenes.scene_goal: 展示付钱的场景，引出关于支付方式的对话。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头切换到收银台。虽然丽莎说要去付钱，但现在站在收银台前的是张天明。一位友善的收银员向他询问支付方式。张天明迅速从钱包里拿出一张信用卡。
scenes.setting_en: A clean, well-lit checkout counter in the department store. A POS machine and shopping bags are on the counter.
scenes.camera_movement: Starts with an over-the-shoulder shot of Zhang Tianming, then cuts to a frontal shot of the cashier. Close-up on Zhang Tianming's hand taking out a credit card.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Hard cut to a department store checkout counter. Zhang Tianming is placing items on the counter. A polite cashier asks him a question. Close-up shot of Zhang Tianming confidently taking a credit card out of his wallet and saying 'I'll use my card'.

voice_over.speaker: 售货员
voice_over.hanzi: 先生,付现金,还是用信用卡?
voice_over.pinyin: xiānsheng fù xiànjīn háishi yòng xìnyòngkǎ
voice_over.translation: Sir, will you be paying with cash or credit card?

hanzi: 先生,加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
pinyin: xiān sheng jiā shàng shuì yí gòng shì yì bǎi bā shí liù kuài sì
translation: Sir, with tax, the total is one hundred eighty-six dollars and forty cents.

scenes.scene_goal: 完成支付流程，结束购物场景。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 收银员操作刷卡机后，告知张天明含税总金额。张天明点点头，接过收银员递来的收据和购物袋，礼貌地道谢告别。
scenes.setting_en: The same checkout counter. The transaction is being finalized.
scenes.camera_movement: Steady medium shot of the interaction. The camera follows the exchange of the credit card, receipt, and shopping bag.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium shot at the checkout counter. The cashier hands a receipt to Zhang Tianming while stating the total. Zhang Tianming nods, takes the receipt and the shopping bag, says thank you with a smile, and turns to leave.

voice_over.speaker: 售货员
voice_over.hanzi: 先生,加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
voice_over.pinyin: xiān sheng jiā shàng shuì yí gòng shì yì bǎi bā shí liù kuài sì
voice_over.translation: Sir, with tax, the total is one hundred eighty-six dollars and forty cents.

global_config.teaching_style: Clear, structured, and encouraging, focusing on practical application.
global_config.visual_style: Clean and modern, using cards and animations to highlight key information.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the background story of why Zhang Tianming needs new clothes and learn how to list examples using '像...什么的'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's start with the first sentence. It sets the scene for our shopping trip. It says: When Zhang Tianming came from home, his mom had bought him some clothes. The sentence then uses the pattern [zh:像...什么的] to list examples of these clothes, such as [zh:T恤衫] (T-shirts), [zh:毛衣] (sweaters), and [zh:牛仔裤] (jeans).

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明从家里来的时候,妈妈给他买了一些衣服,像T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤什么的...
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng cóng jiāli lái de shíhou, māma gěi tā mǎi le yìxiē yīfu, xiàng Tīxùshān, máoyī, niúzǎikù shénme de...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: When Zhang Tianming came from home, his mom had bought him some clothes, like T-shirts, sweaters, jeans, and so on...

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to introduce examples in a list.
A loanword from English 'T-shirt', combining a phonetic part with the Chinese word for shirt, 衫.
Literally 'wool clothing', a common term for sweaters.
Literally 'cowboy pants', the standard word for jeans.
Used at the end of a list of examples to mean 'and so on' or 'etc.'

segments.visual_notes: Animate the clothing items (T-shirt, sweater, jeans) appearing on screen as they are mentioned.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a contrasting opinion using '可是' and the '无论是...都...' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the second part of the first sentence, showing Zhang Tianming's opinion. [zh:可是] means 'but' or 'however'. He feels that, using the structure [zh:无论是...还是...都...], which means 'regardless of whether it's... or...'. So, 'regardless of whether it's the style or the color, neither is very good'. [zh:样子] is style, [zh:颜色] is color, and [zh:不太好] is a polite way to say 'not very good'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是他觉得无论是样子还是颜色都不太好。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: kěshì tā juéde wúlùn shì yàngzi háishi yánsè dōu bú tài hǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: but he felt that neither the style nor the color was very good.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a contrasting idea.
Used in the pattern '无论...都...' to indicate that the result is the same under any condition mentioned.
Refers to the appearance, style, or look of an object.
The word for color.

segments.visual_notes: Visually contrast a 'style' icon and a 'color' icon, then show a 'thumbs down' or similar icon to represent '不太好'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the context of the shopping trip and learn vocabulary for daily necessities.

narration.subtitle_en: The story continues. This sentence explains why the group is going shopping. It starts with 'Today is Sunday'. The word [zh:正好] means 'it just so happens that'. Lin Xuemei and Lisha also [zh:需要] (need) to buy some things. The sentence lists several [zh:日用品], which means 'daily necessities', like toilet paper and toothpaste.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 今天是星期日,正好林雪梅和丽莎也需要买卫生纸、牙膏、毛巾、洗衣粉这些日用品...
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Jīntiān shì xīngqīrì, zhènghǎo Lín Xuěméi hé Lìshā yě xūyào mǎi wèishēngzhǐ, yágāo, máojīn, xǐyīfěn zhèxiē rìyòngpǐn...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Today is Sunday. It just so happens that Lin Xuemei and Lisha also need to buy daily necessities like toilet paper, toothpaste, towels, and laundry detergent...

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb indicating a fortunate coincidence or a perfect time for something.
A verb indicating necessity.
A compound word: 日 (daily) + 用 (use) + 品 (goods).

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for a calendar (on Sunday) and various daily necessities as they are mentioned.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe taking someone to a location and the word for 'shopping center'.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the conclusion of the second sentence. It tells us where they went. [zh:柯林就带他们] means 'So Ke Lin took them'. They went [zh:来到] 'to arrive at' a place. Where? [zh:附近一家最大的购物中心] - 'the biggest shopping center nearby'. [zh:购物中心] is a key term for 'shopping center' or 'mall'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 柯林就带他们来到附近一家最大的购物中心。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kē Lín jiù dài tāmen láidào fùjìn yì jiā zuì dà de gòuwù zhōngxīn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: so Ke Lin took them to the biggest shopping center nearby.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The verb for taking someone or something to a place.
Refers to the vicinity or surrounding area.
A compound word: 购物 (shopping) + 中心 (center).

segments.visual_notes: Show a map animation with a pin dropping on a large building labeled '购物中心'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the basic question pattern for asking someone what they want to buy.

narration.subtitle_en: Now the dialogue begins. A friend asks Zhang Tianming this question. [zh:你] is 'you', [zh:要] means 'want to', [zh:买] is 'buy', [zh:什么] is 'what', and [zh:衣服] is 'clothes'. So, 'What clothes do you want to buy?'. This is a very common and useful question pattern when shopping.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你要买什么衣服?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ yào mǎi shénme yīfu?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What clothes do you want to buy?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates a desire or intention to do something.
The verb for purchasing something.
A general term for clothing.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight each word in the sentence as it's explained.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state what you want to buy, using the correct measure word.

narration.subtitle_en: Here is Zhang Tianming's answer. He says, 'I'd like to buy a tracksuit.' Let's break it down. [zh:我想买] means 'I'd like to buy'. [zh:一套] introduces a measure word, [zh:套], which is used for sets of things, like suits, or in this case, a tracksuit. The item he wants is [zh:运动服], which literally means 'sports clothes'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我想买一套运动服。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng mǎi yí tào yùndòngfú.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I'd like to buy a tracksuit.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Expresses a thought or desire.
A measure word for a set of items, such as a suit of clothes or a set of furniture.
A compound word: 运动 (sports) + 服 (clothing).

segments.visual_notes: Animate a tracksuit appearing next to the text. Emphasize the measure word '套'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for describing clothing and how to talk about discounts.

narration.subtitle_en: The salesperson or a friend points something out. [zh:这边儿就是] means 'They're right over here.' Then they say, 'Take a look at this set'. The description is very positive: [zh:样子、大小、长短都合适]. This means the style, size, and length are all suitable. We see [zh:大小] for size and [zh:长短] for length. Finally, the best part: [zh:而且打八折], 'and what's more, it's 20% off'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这边儿就是。你看看这一套,样子、大小、长短都合适,而且打八折。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhèbiānr jiùshì. Nǐ kànkan zhè yí tào, yàngzi, dàxiǎo, chángduǎn dōu héshì, érqiě dǎ bā zhé.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: They're right over here. Take a look at this set. The style, size, and length are all perfect, and it's 20% off.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An abstract noun for 'size', formed by combining 'big' (大) and 'small' (小).
An abstract noun for 'length', formed by combining 'long' (长) and 'short' (短).
An adjective meaning that something is a good fit.
Indicates that an item is on sale. '打八折' means you pay 80% of the price (20% off).

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for style, size, and length with checkmarks. Animate a '20% OFF' tag appearing next to '打八折'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn essential shopping questions: asking for the price and the brand.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming agrees the color is good: [zh:颜色也不错], 'The color isn't bad either'. Then he asks two important questions for any shopper. First, [zh:多少钱], 'How much is it?'. Second, [zh:是什么牌子的], 'What's the brand?'. The word [zh:牌子] means 'brand'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 颜色也不错。多少钱?是什么牌子的?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yánsè yě búcuò. Duōshao qián? Shì shénme páizi de?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The color isn't bad either. How much is it? What's the brand?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common way to say something is good.
The standard phrase to ask for the price of something.
Refers to the brand name of a product.

segments.visual_notes: Show a price tag icon for '多少钱' and a logo icon for '牌子'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about price and express that you haven't heard of something.

narration.subtitle_en: The friend replies. First, about the price: [zh:价钱不贵], 'The price is not expensive'. [zh:价钱] is another word for 'price'. Then, about the brand: [zh:这个牌子没听说过], 'I haven't heard of this brand'. [zh:听说] means 'to hear someone say' or 'to have heard of', and the particle [zh:过] indicates past experience.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 价钱不贵。这个牌子没听说过。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Jiàqian bù guì. Zhège páizi méi tīngshuō guo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The price is not expensive. I haven't heard of this brand.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A noun for 'price', often interchangeable with '钱' in questions like '多少钱'.
The adjective for 'expensive'.
The verb '听说' (to hear of) combined with the particle '过' to indicate past experience.

segments.visual_notes: Show a price tag with a low number, then a brand logo that fades to a question mark.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to use '不过' to add a contrasting positive point and talk about material.

narration.subtitle_en: The friend adds another piece of information. [zh:不过] means 'however' or 'but'. They point out a good quality: [zh:是纯棉的]. This means 'It is pure cotton'. [zh:纯棉] literally translates to 'pure cotton'. The [zh:是...的] structure is used here to emphasize this characteristic of the material.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 不过,是纯棉的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Búguò, shì chúnmián de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: However, it is pure cotton.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A softer alternative to '可是', used to introduce a contrasting point.
A compound word: 纯 (pure) + 棉 (cotton). Used to describe the material of clothing.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a cotton icon appearing on screen to illustrate '纯棉'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express strong preference and the desire for name brands.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming reveals his preference. He says, [zh:牌子不好不行]. This is a strong statement meaning 'If the brand isn't good, it won't do' or 'It has to be a good brand'. Then he states his goal clearly: [zh:我想买名牌的], 'I want to buy a name brand'. [zh:名牌] means 'famous brand'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 牌子不好不行,我想买名牌的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Páizi bù hǎo bùxíng, wǒ xiǎng mǎi míngpái de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It has to be a good brand. I want to buy a name brand.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to express that something is not acceptable or not possible.
A compound word: 名 (famous) + 牌 (brand).

segments.visual_notes: Show a generic brand logo being crossed out, replaced by several famous-looking logos for '名牌'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn expressions for teasing someone's fashion sense and reacting to a high price.

narration.subtitle_en: His friend teases him a bit. [zh:你真时髦] means 'You're so fashionable!'. Then they spot something: [zh:那件好像是名牌的], 'That one looks like a name brand...'. But then comes the reality check, expressed with the exclamation [zh:哎呀], which is like 'oh my' or 'oops'. The final verdict is [zh:太贵了], 'It's too expensive!'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你真时髦!穿名牌!那件好像是名牌的……哎呀,太贵了!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ zhēn shímáo! Chuān míngpái! Nà jiàn hǎoxiàng shì míngpái de… āiyā, tài guì le!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You're so fashionable! Wearing name brands! That one looks like a name brand... Oh my, it's too expensive!

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adjective to describe someone or something as being in style.
Used to express an impression or likeness.
An interjection used to express surprise or shock.
A structure used to express 'too' much of a quality, as in '太贵了' (too expensive).

segments.visual_notes: Animate a sparkle effect for '时髦', then a price tag that rapidly increases to a high number for '太贵了'.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand a preference for name-brand items and the reasoning behind it.

narration.subtitle_en: In this line, the speaker states a strong preference for shopping. They say, [zh:买东西,我只买名牌的]. This means 'When I go shopping, I only buy name-brand items.' The word [zh:只] means 'only'. Then, they add [zh:要不然就不买], 'otherwise I don't buy at all.' The reason is given in the last part: [zh:因为名牌的衣服质量好], 'because name-brand clothes are better quality.' This sets up a discussion about shopping values.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 买东西,我只买名牌的,要不然就不买,因为名牌的衣服质量好。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Mǎi dōngxi, wǒ zhǐ mǎi míngpái de, yàoburán jiù bù mǎi, yīnwèi míngpái de yīfu zhìliàng hǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: When I shop, I only buy name brands, otherwise I don't buy at all, because name-brand clothes are better quality.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A famous or well-known brand. Literally 'famous plate/card'.
A conjunction used to introduce a consequence if the preceding condition is not met.
Refers to the quality of a product or thing. In Taiwan, '品质' (pǐnzhí) is more common.

segments.visual_notes: Animate icons for 'shopping bag', 'brand tag', and 'thumbs up for quality'.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the argument that buying cheap items can be more expensive in the long run.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, the second speaker agrees and elaborates. They start with [zh:不错], 'That's right.' Then comes a key pattern: [zh:有的衣服便宜是便宜,可是牌子不好]. This means 'Some clothes are cheap, it's true, but the brand isn't good.' The structure 'Adjective + 是 + Adjective' is used to acknowledge a point before introducing a contrasting one. Because the quality is poor, [zh:穿一、两次就不想穿了], 'after you wear them once or twice, you don't want to wear them anymore.' The result? [zh:只好再买一件], 'so you have no choice but to buy another one.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 不错。有的衣服便宜是便宜,可是牌子不好,穿一、两次就不想穿了,只好再买一件。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Búcuò. Yǒude yīfu piányi shì piányi, kěshì páizi bù hǎo, chuān yī, liǎng cì jiù bù xiǎng chuān le, zhǐhǎo zài mǎi yí jiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That's right. Some clothes are cheap, it's true, but the brand isn't good. After you wear them once or twice, you don't want to wear them anymore, so you have to buy another one.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure to express partial agreement before presenting a contrast.
Indicates that there are no other options available.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of a t-shirt looking great, then quickly fading and tearing after one wash.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the comparison being made about the total cost.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the conclusion of the previous thought. The speaker says, [zh:这样买两件衣服的钱]. This phrase means 'The money for buying two pieces of clothing this way'. The word [zh:这样] means 'in this way'. The sentence then makes a comparison: [zh:可能比买一件名牌的更多]. This means it 'might be more than buying one name-brand item.' The key comparison word here is [zh:比], which works like 'than' in English. So, two cheap items might cost more than one expensive, quality one.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这样买两件衣服的钱可能比买一件名牌的更多。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhèyàng mǎi liǎng jiàn yīfu de qián kěnéng bǐ mǎi yí jiàn míngpái de gèng duō.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The money you spend on two pieces of clothing might be more than buying one name-brand item.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to the manner or method just described.
The core structure for making comparisons: A 比 B + Adjective.
Used in comparisons to mean 'even more' or simply 'more'.

segments.visual_notes: Use a scale animation: two cheap shirts on one side, one name-brand shirt on the other. The side with two shirts tips down, showing it's 'heavier' in cost.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to agree with someone's reasoning using a common phrase.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a short and very useful phrase for agreeing with someone. [zh:你说的有道理]. Let's break it down. [zh:你说的] means 'what you said'. [zh:有] means 'to have'. And [zh:道理] means 'reason' or 'sense'. So, all together, it literally means 'What you said has reason,' which we translate as 'What you said makes sense.' It's a great way to show you're following the conversation and agree with the logic.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你说的有道理。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de yǒu dàoli.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What you said makes sense.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common phrase used to concur with someone's opinion or logic. Literally 'has reason'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a speech bubble with the previous line, then a checkmark or a nodding head icon appears next to it.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand a counterargument about shopping criteria, focusing on the structure '不一定非...不可'.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, a new perspective is introduced. The speaker starts by partially agreeing: [zh:买衣服只考虑便宜当然不好], 'Of course it's not good to only consider price when buying clothes.' But then they pivot: [zh:但是也不一定非买名牌的不可]. This is a key phrase. [zh:非...不可] means 'must' or 'have to'. By adding [zh:不一定] 'not necessarily' in front, the whole phrase means 'you don't necessarily have to buy name brands.' It's a polite way to disagree.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 买衣服只考虑便宜当然不好,但是也不一定非买名牌的不可。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Mǎi yīfu zhǐ kǎolǜ piányi dāngrán bù hǎo, dànshì yě bù yídìng fēi mǎi míngpái de bù kě.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Of course it's not good to only consider price when buying clothes, but you don't necessarily have to buy name brands.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To think about something carefully before making a decision.
Used to express uncertainty or that something is not always the case.
A strong expression indicating that something is absolutely necessary or unavoidable.

segments.visual_notes: Show a split screen. On one side, a price tag. On the other, a brand logo. A big 'X' appears over the idea of 'only' one or the other.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to list criteria using '第一, 第二, 第三' and the A-not-A question form.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker then lists their personal criteria. They start with [zh:我买衣服的标准], 'My criteria for buying clothes'. [zh:标准] means 'standard' or 'criteria'. Then they list them out: [zh:第一是看穿着舒服不舒服], 'First is whether they're comfortable to wear'. Notice the 'A-not-A' form [zh:舒服不舒服]. The list continues: [zh:第二是质量好不好], 'Second, whether the quality is good,' and [zh:第三是价钱合适不合适], 'Third, whether the price is suitable.' This is a very clear way to organize and present your points.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我买衣服的标准,第一是看穿着舒服不舒服,第二是质量好不好,第三是价钱合适不合适。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ mǎi yīfu de biāozhǔn, dìyī shì kàn chuānzhe shūfu bù shūfu, dì'èr shì zhìliàng hǎo bù hǎo, dìsān shì jiàqian héshì bù héshì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My criteria for buying clothes are, first, whether they're comfortable, second, whether the quality is good, and third, whether the price is suitable.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Can be a noun for 'standard' or an adjective for 'standardized'.
Describes a state of physical or mental ease.
Describes something that fits a purpose, situation, or person well.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a checklist with three items: 1. Comfort (a pillow icon), 2. Quality (a diamond icon), 3. Price (a money bag icon).

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to express indifference using '在乎' in a negative sentence.

narration.subtitle_en: Continuing the thought, the speaker lists things they are not concerned with. [zh:是什么牌子的], 'what the brand is,' [zh:样子时髦不时髦], 'whether the style is fashionable or not.' The word [zh:时髦] means 'fashionable'. The key takeaway is the final phrase: [zh:我都不在乎]. The word [zh:在乎] means 'to care about' or 'to mind'. Used in the negative with [zh:都], it means 'I don't care about any of them at all.' It's a strong statement of indifference.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是什么牌子的,样子时髦不时髦,我都不在乎。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: shì shénme páizi de, yàngzi shímáo bù shímáo, wǒ dōu bú zàihu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I don't care what the brand is, or whether the style is fashionable or not.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Describes something that is currently in style.
Often used in negative sentences to express that one doesn't mind something.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for a brand logo and a flashy shirt, then have a large shrug emoji or a hand wave them away.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the '为了...不是为了...' structure to explain motivation.

narration.subtitle_en: Finally, the speaker explains their philosophy. [zh:因为穿衣服是为了自己], 'Because you wear clothes for yourself'. The structure [zh:为了] means 'for the sake of' or 'in order to'. This is contrasted with the next part: [zh:不是为了给别人看], 'not for others to see.' The full structure '...is for A, not for B' is a powerful way to state your primary motivation and what you disregard. It's a very personal and confident statement.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 因为穿衣服是为了自己,不是为了给别人看。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yīnwèi chuān yīfu shì wèile zìjǐ, búshì wèile gěi biérén kàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Because you wear clothes for yourself, not for others to see.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates the purpose or goal of an action.
A reflexive pronoun, referring back to the subject.
Refers to people other than oneself.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a person looking happily in a mirror, then contrast with an image of a person looking for approval from a crowd.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to express disagreement and pose a challenging question.

narration.subtitle_en: The first speaker is not convinced. They state their position very directly: [zh:我不同意], 'I disagree.' [zh:同意] means 'to agree,' so adding [zh:不] negates it. To challenge the other person's philosophy, they ask a personal, rhetorical question: [zh:你喜欢看雪梅穿不好看的衣服吗], 'Do you like to see Xuemei wearing clothes that don't look good?' This shifts the debate from abstract principles to a concrete, personal example.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我不同意。你喜欢看雪梅穿不好看的衣服吗?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ bù tóngyì. Nǐ xǐhuān kàn Xuěméi chuān bù hǎokàn de yīfu ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I disagree. Do you like to see Xuemei wearing clothes that don't look good?

highlight_words.explanation_en: To share the same opinion or to give consent.
The negative form of 'hǎokàn' (good-looking).

segments.visual_notes: Show a big red 'X' over the previous speaker's statement, followed by a question mark pointing at a character.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand how to use a compliment and a tag question to evade a direct answer.

narration.subtitle_en: Instead of answering the difficult question directly, the speaker pays a compliment. [zh:雪梅穿什么衣服都好看], 'Xuemei looks good in any clothes.' This uses the 'question word + [zh:都]' structure. [zh:什么...都] means 'whatever' or 'any'. Then, they turn to the group for support with a tag question: [zh:对不对], 'Right?' This is a clever way to deflect the argument by flattering someone and seeking consensus.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 雪梅穿什么衣服都好看,对不对?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xuěméi chuān shénme yīfu dōu hǎokàn, duì bu duì?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Xuemei looks good in any clothes, right?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A pattern used to mean 'any' or 'all' in a given category.
Literally 'good to look at', used for people or things.
A common tag question to seek confirmation, similar to '..., right?' in English.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character trying on different outfits (a hat, a scarf, glasses) and looking great in all of them.

segments.teaching_goal: Recognize a phrase used to stop a conversation or playful argument.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a quick, informal response to end the teasing. The speaker says [zh:好,好,好], 'Okay, okay, okay,' showing they are conceding or just want to move on. This is immediately followed by [zh:别说了], which means 'Stop talking' or 'Don't say any more.' It's a common way to playfully or seriously shut down a topic.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 好,好,好,别说了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hǎo, hǎo, hǎo, bié shuō le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Okay, okay, okay, stop talking.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A command telling someone to stop speaking.

segments.visual_notes: Show a hand gesture for 'stop' or a zipper closing over a mouth.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand how to point out a seeming contradiction using '怎么' and '不是...吗?'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, the topic shifts back to Ke Lin. The speaker starts with [zh:哎] to get his attention. They point out, [zh:你身上这件衣服怎么是名牌的]. The word [zh:怎么] here expresses surprise, like 'how come'. So, 'How come the shirt you're wearing is a name brand?' Then they ask a rhetorical question: [zh:你不是不穿名牌的吗], 'Don't you not wear name brands?' or 'I thought you didn't wear name brands.' This [zh:不是...吗] question implies the speaker is surprised by this new information which contradicts what they previously believed.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎,柯林,你身上这件衣服怎么是名牌的,你不是不穿名牌的吗?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āi, Kē Lín, nǐ shēnshang zhè jiàn yīfu zěnme shì míngpái de, nǐ búshì bù chuān míngpái de ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hey, Ke Lin, how come the shirt you're wearing is a name brand? Don't you not wear name brands?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to something on a person, usually clothing or accessories.
Used here to express surprise or ask for a reason in a questioning tone.
A rhetorical question structure used to express surprise or confirm an assumption.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a magnifying glass zooming in on the brand logo on Ke Lin's shirt, with a large question mark appearing.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to clarify a previous statement and provide an exception.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin defends himself by clarifying his original point. He says, [zh:我说不一定非买名牌的不可], 'I said you don't necessarily have to buy name brands.' He then contrasts this with what he didn't say: [zh:没说过不穿名牌呀], 'I never said I don't wear them!' The particle [zh:呀] at the end softens the tone. He provides the reason for this exception: [zh:这件是打折的时候买的], 'I bought this one when it was on sale.' The key word [zh:打折] means 'on sale' or 'discounted'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我说不一定非买名牌的不可,没说过不穿名牌呀。这件是打折的时候买的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ shuō bù yídìng fēi mǎi míngpái de bù kě, méi shuōguo bù chuān míngpái ya. Zhè jiàn shì dǎzhé de shíhou mǎi de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I said you don't necessarily have to buy name brands, I never said I don't wear them. I bought this one when it was on sale.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The negative past experience form of 'shuō' (to say).

highlight_words.translation: to be on sale, to discount
highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that an item is sold at a reduced price.

segments.visual_notes: Show a price tag with the original price crossed out and a new, lower price next to a 'SALE' sign.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to suggest moving to another area or activity.

narration.subtitle_en: To change the subject and move the group along, the speaker says, [zh:哎,丽莎], 'Hey, Lisha,' to get her attention. Then they make a suggestion: [zh:我们去日用品那边看看]. This means 'Let's go check out the daily necessities section.' [zh:日用品] are 'daily necessities'—things like toothpaste and towels. The phrase [zh:去...看看] is a very common and casual way to suggest going somewhere to have a look.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎,丽莎,我们去日用品那边看看。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āi, Lìshā, wǒmen qù rìyòngpǐn nàbiān kànkan.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hey, Lisha, let's go check out the daily necessities section.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Items used in daily life, such as toiletries and cleaning supplies. Literally 'daily use items'.
A location word meaning 'that side' or 'over there'.
Reduplication of the verb 'kàn' (to look), which softens the tone and implies a brief or casual action.

segments.visual_notes: Animate an arrow pointing towards a different section of the store labeled with icons for toothpaste, soap, and towels.

segments.segment_title: 你们去吧,我先去付钱,我们一会儿见。
segments.teaching_goal: To understand and use phrases for temporary parting and stating one's immediate plan.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming tells his friends to go on without him. This sentence has three parts. First, [zh:你们去吧] means 'You guys go ahead.' The particle [zh:吧] softens the suggestion, making it friendly. Next, [zh:我先去付钱] means 'I'll go pay first.' Here, [zh:先] means 'first,' and the key verb is [zh:付钱], 'to pay money.' Finally, [zh:我们一会儿见] means 'I'll see you in a bit.' This is a very common way to part temporarily with someone you expect to see again soon.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们去吧,我先去付钱,我们一会儿见。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen qù ba, wǒ xiān qù fùqián, wǒmen yíhuìr jiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You guys go ahead. I'm going to pay first. I'll see you in a bit.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase meaning 'to pay money.' [zh:付] means 'to pay' and [zh:钱] means 'money'.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: The character 付 (fù) is composed of a person radical 亻 and 寸 (cùn), an ancient unit of length, suggesting handing something over. 钱 (qián) means money.

highlight_words.translation: in a moment, a little while
highlight_words.explanation_en: A noun that indicates a short period of time. It's often used to say 'see you in a bit' ([zh:一会儿见]).

segments.visual_notes: Animate the sentence breaking into three distinct clauses, highlighting each part as it's explained.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand that text in parentheses indicates a stage direction and to recognize the '在 + Verb' structure for ongoing actions.

narration.subtitle_en: This line is in parentheses, which tells us it's a stage direction describing the scene, not spoken dialogue. It says [zh:张天明在付钱]. The structure [zh:在] plus a verb, like [zh:在付钱], indicates an ongoing action, similar to 'is paying' in English. The ellipsis at the end suggests the action continues as the scene transitions to the checkout counter.

on_screen_text.focus_text: (张天明在付钱……)
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: (Zhāng Tiānmíng zài fùqián…)
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: (Zhang Tianming is at the checkout...)
on_screen_text.notes: Parentheses (...) indicate an action or scene description.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used before a verb, 'zài' indicates that an action is in progress.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the parentheses and the character '在' to emphasize their functions.

segments.segment_title: 先生,付现金,还是用信用卡?
segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask about payment methods using the 'A 还是 B' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: Now we hear the cashier speaking. They start with [zh:先生], a polite term for 'Sir' or 'Mister.' Then they offer payment options using the 'A or B' question structure, which is [zh:A 还是 B]. The two options here are [zh:付现金], which means 'to pay with cash,' and [zh:用信用卡], 'to use a credit card.' This is a classic example of an alternative question you'll hear frequently when shopping.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 先生,付现金,还是用信用卡?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xiānsheng, fù xiànjīn, háishì yòng xìnyòngkǎ?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Sir, will you be paying with cash or credit card?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'present gold,' this word means cash.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 现 (xiàn) means 'present' or 'current'. 金 (jīn) means 'gold' or 'money'. Together, they refer to physical currency on hand.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compound word for 'credit card'.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 信用 (xìnyòng) means 'credit' or 'trustworthiness'. 卡 (kǎ) is a loanword from 'card'. So, a 'credit card'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used in questions to offer a choice between two or more options.

segments.visual_notes: Visually separate the two choices '付现金' and '用信用卡' on either side of '还是'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the standard phrase for paying by card.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming gives a short, direct answer: [zh:我刷卡]. This literally means 'I swipe card.' The verb [zh:刷] means 'to brush' or 'to swipe.' In the context of payment, [zh:刷卡] is the standard, everyday phrase for paying by card, whether it's a credit or debit card. It's a very useful and common phrase to remember for any shopping situation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我刷卡。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ shuā kǎ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I'll use my card.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase that means 'to swipe a card,' used for paying with any bank card.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 刷 (shuā) means 'to brush' or 'swipe'. 卡 (kǎ) is from the English 'card'. The combination vividly describes the action of paying with a card.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of a card swiping through a reader as the phrase '刷卡' is highlighted.

segments.segment_title: 先生,加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
segments.teaching_goal: To understand how prices, including tax, are stated in Chinese.

narration.subtitle_en: The cashier states the final price. Let's break it down. [zh:加上税] means 'adding tax' or 'with tax included.' Then, [zh:一共是] means 'the total is.' The price itself is [zh:一百八十六块四]. In spoken Chinese, [zh:块] is the common word for the main currency unit, like 'dollars' or 'yuan.' The final [zh:四] here is shorthand for 'four jiao' or forty cents. So the total is 186.40.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 先生,加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xiānsheng, jiāshàng shuì yígòng shì yìbǎi bāshíliù kuài sì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Sir, with tax, the total is one hundred eighty-six dollars and forty cents.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Means 'to add' or 'plus.' Here it means 'including' the tax.
The word for tax.
Used to state the total sum of something.
A colloquial measure word for the basic unit of Chinese currency, the yuan.

segments.visual_notes: Display the price '186.40' on screen. As the narration explains '块' and '四', highlight the corresponding digits.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the standard polite phrases for concluding a transaction.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the end of the transaction. Zhang Tianming's response is simple and polite. He starts with [zh:好], which works as 'Okay' or 'Alright' to acknowledge the total. The ellipsis suggests a brief pause. He then says the essential polite phrases you should always use: [zh:谢谢] for 'Thank you,' and [zh:再见] for 'Goodbye.' It's a simple and effective way to end the interaction.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 好……谢谢!再见。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hǎo… Xièxie! Zàijiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Okay... Thank you! Goodbye.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Here, it's used as an affirmative response, similar to 'Okay' or 'Alright'.

segments.visual_notes: Quickly flash the words '好', '谢谢', and '再见' on screen as they are mentioned.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach students how to use '无论...都...' to express that a result remains the same regardless of different conditions or choices.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's dive into our first grammar point: [zh:无论...都...]. This structure means 'regardless of' or 'no matter what'. It's used to say that a certain outcome won't change, even if the circumstances do. You'll always use it with a question word like 'who', 'what', or 'where', or with an alternative choice like 'A or B'. The second part of the sentence must almost always include [zh:都]. In our dialogue, Zhang Tianming felt that [zh:无论是样子还是颜色都不太好], meaning 'regardless of whether it was the style or the color, neither was very good.' The condition is the 'style or color', and the unchanging result is that he 'didn't like it'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 无论 + [Question Word / A还是B], ...都...
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: wúlùn + [Question Word / A háishì B], ...dōu...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Regardless of... / No matter..., [result] is always...
on_screen_text.notes: The main clause must contain an adverb, usually 都 (dōu), to emphasize the invariable result.

grammar_points.pattern: 无论是样子还是颜色都不太好。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: wúlùn shì yàngzi háishì yánsè dōu bú tài hǎo.
grammar_points.explanation_en: From the dialogue: 'Regardless of whether it was the style or the color, neither was very good.'

grammar_points.pattern: 明天无论谁请客我都不去。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Míngtiān wúlùn shéi qǐngkè wǒ dōu bú qù.
grammar_points.explanation_en: It doesn't matter who's paying tomorrow. I'm not going.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the structure, showing the 'condition' part feeding into the 'unchanging result' part.

segments.teaching_goal: To explain adjectives that are mainly used before nouns and how to use them as predicates with '是...的'.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's talk about a special type of adjective called non-predicative adjectives. These words, like [zh:纯棉] for 'pure cotton', usually just describe a noun's inherent quality and go before it, like '[zh:纯棉] T-shirt'. You can't say something is 'very [zh:纯棉]'. So, how do you say 'This is pure cotton'? You need the structure [zh:是...的]. In the dialogue, Zhang Tianming notes that the tracksuit [zh:是纯棉的], 'is pure cotton'. This structure helps categorize the item. Other examples include [zh:男] for male and [zh:女] for female, as in '[zh:他是男的]' (He is male).

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是 + [Non-predicative Adjective] + 的
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: shì + [Non-predicative Adjective] + de
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Used to make a non-predicative adjective the main description of a sentence.
on_screen_text.notes: Example from text: 不过, 是纯棉的。 (However, it is pure cotton.)

grammar_points.pattern: 我穿的这件衬衫是纯棉的。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ chuān de zhè jiàn chènshān shì chúnmián de.
grammar_points.explanation_en: This shirt I'm wearing is one-hundred-percent cotton.

grammar_points.pattern: 小李买的水果都是有机的。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Xiǎo Lǐ mǎi de shuǐguǒ dōu shì yǒujī de.
grammar_points.explanation_en: The fruit Little Li buys is all organic.

segments.visual_notes: Show a list of non-predicative adjectives (男, 女, 纯棉, 有机). Then show one being placed into the '是...的' sentence frame.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the structure 'Adjective/Verb + 是 + Adjective/Verb, 可是/但是...' for expressing partial agreement.

narration.subtitle_en: How do you agree with someone, but also add a counterpoint? Use the structure '[zh:...是..., 可是...]'. It means 'It's true that..., but...'. You repeat the adjective or verb to acknowledge the other person's point before introducing your own perspective with [zh:可是] or [zh:但是]. Lisha uses this in the dialogue: [zh:有的衣服便宜是便宜, 可是牌子不好]. She's saying, 'It's true some clothes are cheap, but the brand isn't good.' This is a very common and polite way to express a nuanced opinion.

on_screen_text.focus_text: [Adj./Verb] + 是 + [Adj./Verb], 可是...
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: [Adj./Verb] + shì + [Adj./Verb], kěshì...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It is true that..., but...
on_screen_text.notes: This structure acknowledges a fact before presenting a contrasting point.

grammar_points.pattern: 有的衣服便宜是便宜, 可是牌子不好。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: yǒu de yī fu pián yi shì pián yi, kě shì pái zi bù hǎo.
grammar_points.explanation_en: From the dialogue: 'Some clothes are cheap, it's true, but the brand isn't good.'

grammar_points.pattern: 这件衣服贵是贵, 可是牌子好。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zhè jiàn yīfu guì shì guì, kěshì páizi hǎo.
grammar_points.explanation_en: It is expensive, but it's a good brand.

segments.visual_notes: Use a scale visual. First, one side goes down (agreement), then the other side goes down (the 'but' part), showing a balanced view.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach students to use '什么的' to end a list of examples, similar to 'and so on' or 'etc.'

narration.subtitle_en: When you list a few items and want to say 'and so on' or 'and stuff like that', you can use [zh:什么的]. It's a casual way to indicate that your list is not exhaustive. In the first line, the narrator mentions the clothes Zhang Tianming's mom bought for him: [zh:像T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤什么的]. This means 'like T-shirts, sweaters, jeans, and so on.' You just list a few examples and then add [zh:什么的] at the end. It's simple and very useful in daily conversation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: [Item 1], [Item 2], ... 什么的
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: [Item 1], [Item 2], ... shénme de
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: ... and so on; ... and what not; etc.
on_screen_text.notes: Used to cap a series of items.

grammar_points.pattern: 他要买毛衣、牛仔裤什么的。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Tā yào mǎi máoyī, niúzǎikù shénme de.
grammar_points.explanation_en: He wants to buy sweaters, jeans, and so on.

grammar_points.pattern: 他床上放了很多东西，有被子、毯子、衣服什么的。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Tā chuángshang fàngle hěn duō dōngxi, yǒu bèizi, tǎnzi, yīfu shénme de.
grammar_points.explanation_en: There's lots of stuff on his bed: a quilt, a blanket, clothes, and so on.

segments.visual_notes: Show a list of items appearing one by one, ending with '什么的' and a '...' icon.

segments.segment_title: Grammar: 大小, 长短 (Abstract Nouns)
segments.teaching_goal: To explain how opposite adjectives like 'big/small' and 'long/short' combine to form abstract nouns.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's look at a clever feature of Chinese: forming abstract nouns by combining opposites. For example, take [zh:大] (big) and [zh:小] (small). Put them together, and you get [zh:大小], which means 'size'. Similarly, [zh:长] (long) and [zh:短] (short) combine to make [zh:长短], meaning 'length'. In the dialogue, Ke Lin says the tracksuit's [zh:样子、大小、长短都合适], meaning its 'style, size, and length are all suitable'. This is a common way to refer to these abstract qualities.

on_screen_text.focus_text:
大 + 小 → 大小 (size)
长 + 短 → 长短 (length)
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin:
dà + xiǎo → dàxiǎo
cháng + duǎn → chángduǎn
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Opposite adjectives can combine to form abstract nouns.
on_screen_text.notes: Example from text: ...样子、大小、长短都合适。

grammar_points.pattern: 这条裤子长短正好。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zhè tiáo kùzi chángduǎn zhènghǎo.
grammar_points.explanation_en: The length of these pants is just right.

segments.visual_notes: Show two characters like '大' and '小' merging to form '大小'.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the Chinese system for expressing discounts, where the number indicates the percentage of the price to be paid.

narration.subtitle_en: Shopping in China, you'll see [zh:打折] everywhere, which means 'on sale'. But the system is different from English. The number tells you what percentage of the original price you PAY. So, [zh:打八折], as seen in the dialogue, means you pay 80%, which is a 20% discount. [zh:打七折] means you pay 70%, a 30% discount. A 50% discount is called [zh:打五折] or [zh:打对折], which literally means 'fold in half'. Always remember: the smaller the number, the bigger the discount!

content.title: Understanding Discounts (打折)
content.note: The number indicates the percentage of the original price you pay.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the use of '(要)不然' to introduce a negative consequence that will happen if a suggestion is not followed.

narration.subtitle_en: The word [zh:要不然] means 'otherwise'. It's used to present a negative consequence if the advice or condition in the first part of the sentence isn't met. The [zh:要] is optional. In the dialogue, Zhang Tianming states his shopping philosophy: [zh:我只买名牌的, 要不然就不买]. This means, 'I only buy name brands, otherwise I don't buy at all.' The structure is: first, state the condition or suggestion, then use [zh:要不然] to explain the undesirable alternative.

on_screen_text.focus_text: [Condition / Suggestion], (要)不然 [Negative Consequence].
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: [Condition / Suggestion], (yào)burán [Negative Consequence].
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: ..., otherwise ...
on_screen_text.notes: Expresses a dire consequence if the advice isn't taken.

grammar_points.pattern: 你得再买一条毯子，要不然容易感冒。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Nǐ děi zài mǎi yì tiáo tǎnzi, yàoburán róngyì gǎnmào.
grammar_points.explanation_en: You've got to buy another blanket; otherwise, you'll catch cold easily.

segments.visual_notes: Show a forked path. One path follows the suggestion and leads to a good outcome. The other path, labeled '要不然', leads to a bad outcome.

segments.segment_title: Grammar: 只好 (Have no choice but to)
segments.teaching_goal: To teach '只好' to express that there are no other options available, forcing a particular course of action.

narration.subtitle_en: What do you do when you have no other choice? You use [zh:只好]. This word means 'to have no choice but to' or 'can only'. It implies that the situation has limited your options, and you're left with only one, often reluctant, course of action. Lisha explains that if a cheap shirt is low quality, after wearing it once or twice, you don't want to wear it anymore, so you [zh:只好再买一件] 'have no choice but to buy another one.' It highlights a forced decision due to circumstances.

on_screen_text.focus_text: [Situation], 只好 [Forced Action]
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: [Situation], zhǐhǎo [Forced Action]
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: ..., have no choice but to ...
on_screen_text.notes: Denotes the nonexistence of other options.

grammar_points.pattern: 我的车让妹妹开走了, 我只好坐公共汽车去上班。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ de chē ràng mèimei kāi zǒu le, wǒ zhǐhǎo zuò gōnggòng qìchē qù shàngbān.
grammar_points.explanation_en: My younger sister drove my car away. I had to go to work by bus.

segments.visual_notes: Show several doors closing, leaving only one door open labeled '只好'.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the '非...不可' structure for expressing strong necessity, determination, or insistence.

narration.subtitle_en: To express strong determination or that something is an absolute must, you can use the structure [zh:非...不可]. It can mean 'insist on' or 'have to'. Lin Xuemei says one doesn't [zh:非买名牌的不可], meaning 'necessarily have to buy name brands.' This shows it can be used in the negative to mean 'don't have to'. It can also show strong personal preference, like 'When he eats fish, he [zh:非吃清蒸的不可]' - 'he insists on having it steamed.' It's a very strong and emphatic expression.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 非 + [Verb/Noun] + 不可
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: fēi + [Verb/Noun] + bùkě
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: must; have to; insist on
on_screen_text.notes: Expresses strong resolution or a necessary condition.

grammar_points.pattern: 张天明买衣服非买名牌的不可。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng mǎi yīfu fēi mǎi míngpái de bù kě.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Zhang Tianming insists on buying only designer clothes.

grammar_points.pattern: 你得的这个病非打针不可。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Nǐ dé de zhège bìng fēi dǎzhēn bù kě.
grammar_points.explanation_en: For this illness, you have no choice but to get an injection.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character with a determined face pointing emphatically at an object, with '非...不可' text appearing.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the word '标准' and its use as both a noun ('standard') and an adjective ('standard').

narration.subtitle_en: The word [zh:标准] is very versatile. It can be a noun meaning 'standard' or 'criterion'. Lin Xuemei uses it this way when she lists her [zh:买衣服的标准], 'criteria for buying clothes'. But [zh:标准] can also be an adjective. You could say someone speaks [zh:标准的北京话], 'standard Beijing dialect'. Or you could say your pronunciation [zh:不太标准], 'is not very standard'. So it can be a thing—a standard—or it can describe something as being standard.

on_screen_text.focus_text:
1. Noun: 标准 (criterion/standard)
2. Adjective: 标准 (standard)
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: biāozhǔn
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: A versatile word for 'standard'.
on_screen_text.notes: Example from text: 我买衣服的标准... (My criteria for buying clothes...)

grammar_points.pattern: Q: 你认为一个好老师的标准是什么? (Noun)

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the verb '在乎', emphasizing its common use in negative sentences to mean 'don't care'.

narration.subtitle_en: Our final grammar point is the verb [zh:在乎], which means 'to care about' or 'to mind'. While you can use it in positive sentences, it's extremely common in negative ones. Lin Xuemei expresses her philosophy by saying that for brand and style, [zh:我都不在乎], 'I don't care about any of it.' This is a great phrase to know when you want to express indifference. You can say [zh:我不在乎] for 'I don't care.' It shows what is and isn't important to you.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 在乎 (to care about) / 不在乎 (to not care about)
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: zàihu / bú zàihu
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Often used in the negative to express indifference.
on_screen_text.notes: Example from text: ...我都不在乎。 (...I don't care about any of it.)

grammar_points.pattern: 别人说什么,我不在乎。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Biérén shuō shénme, wǒ bú zàihu.
grammar_points.explanation_en: I don't care what other people say.

grammar_points.pattern: 他很在乎别人怎么看他。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Tā hěn zàihu biérén zěnme kàn tā.
grammar_points.explanation_en: He cares a lot how other people see him.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character shrugging with the text '我不在乎' appearing in a speech bubble.

segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre le contexte expliquant pourquoi Zhang Tianming a besoin de nouveaux vêtements et apprendre à énumérer des exemples en utilisant '像...什么的'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Commençons par la première phrase. Elle plante le décor de notre virée shopping. Elle dit : « Quand Zhang Tianming est rentré chez lui, sa mère lui avait acheté des vêtements. » La phrase utilise ensuite le modèle [zh:像...什么的] pour énumérer des exemples de ces vêtements, comme des [zh:T恤衫], des T-shirts, des [zh:毛衣], des pulls, et des [zh:牛仔裤], des jeans.

content.focus_text: 张天明从家里来的时候,妈妈给他买了一些衣服,像T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤什么的...
content.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng cóng jiāli lái de shíhou, māma gěi tā mǎi le yìxiē yīfu, xiàng Tīxùshān, máoyī, niúzǎikù shénme de...
content.focus_gloss_en: Quand Zhang Tianming est arrivé de chez lui, sa mère lui avait acheté des vêtements, comme des T-shirts, des pulls, des jeans, et ainsi de suite...

highlight_words.explanation_en: Utilisé pour introduire des exemples dans une liste.
Un emprunt de l'anglais 'T-shirt', combinant une partie phonétique avec le mot chinois pour chemise, 衫.
Littéralement 'vêtement en laine', un terme courant pour les pulls.
Utilisé pour introduire des exemples dans une liste.
Un emprunt au mot anglais 'T-shirt', combinant une partie phonétique avec le mot chinois pour chemise, 衫.
Littéralement 'vêtement en laine', un terme courant pour les pulls.
Littéralement 'pantalon de cow-boy', le mot standard pour les jeans.
Utilisé à la fin d'une liste d'exemples pour signifier « et ainsi de suite » ou « etc. ».

segments.visual_notes: Animer les articles vestimentaires (T-shirt, pull, jean) qui apparaissent à l'écran au fur et à mesure qu'ils sont mentionnés.

segments.teaching_goal: Apprendre à exprimer une opinion contrastée en utilisant '可是' et la structure '无论是...都...'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: C'est la deuxième partie de la première phrase, qui exprime l'opinion de Zhang Tianming. [zh:可是] signifie « mais » ou « cependant ». Il estime que, avec la structure [zh:无论是...还是...都...], cela veut dire « que ce soit... ou... ». Donc, « que ce soit le style ou la couleur, rien n'est très bien ». [zh:样子] signifie « style », et [zh:颜色] signifie « couleur ». [zh:不太好] est une façon polie de dire « pas très bien ».

content.focus_text: 可是他觉得无论是样子还是颜色都不太好。
content.focus_pinyin: kěshì tā juéde wúlùn shì yàngzi háishi yánsè dōu bú tài hǎo.
content.focus_gloss_en: mais il a trouvé que ni le style ni la couleur n'étaient très bons.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Une conjonction utilisée pour introduire une idée contrastée.
Utilisé dans la structure '无论是...都...' pour indiquer que le résultat est le même quelles que soient les conditions mentionnées.
Fait référence à l'apparence, au style ou à l'allure d'un objet.
Une conjonction utilisée pour introduire une idée contrastée.
Utilisé dans la structure '无论是...都...' pour indiquer que le résultat est le même quelles que soient les conditions mentionnées.
Fait référence à l'apparence, au style ou à l'allure d'un objet.
Le mot pour couleur.

segments.segment_title: Une occasion de faire du shopping
segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre le contexte de la sortie shopping et apprendre le vocabulaire des nécessités quotidiennes.

narration_track.subtitle_en: L'histoire continue. Cette phrase explique pourquoi le groupe va faire du shopping. Elle commence par « Aujourd'hui, c'est dimanche ». Le mot [zh:正好] signifie « il se trouve que ». Lin Xuemei et Lisha ont aussi [zh:需要], besoin, d'acheter des choses. La phrase énumère plusieurs [zh:日用品], ce qui signifie « nécessités quotidiennes », comme du papier toilette et du dentifrice.

content.focus_text: 今天是星期日,正好林雪梅和丽莎也需要买卫生纸、牙膏、毛巾、洗衣粉这些日用品...
content.focus_pinyin: Jīntiān shì xīngqīrì, zhènghǎo Lín Xuěméi hé Lìshā yě xūyào mǎi wèishēngzhǐ, yágāo, máojīn, xǐyīfěn zhèxiē rìyòngpǐn...
content.focus_gloss_en: Aujourd'hui, c'est dimanche. Il se trouve que Lin Xuemei et Lisha ont aussi besoin d'acheter des produits de première nécessité comme du papier toilette, du dentifrice, des serviettes et de la lessive...

highlight_words.translation: par coïncidence, il se trouve que
highlight_words.explanation_en: Un adverbe qui indique une coïncidence heureuse ou un moment parfait pour quelque chose.
Un verbe qui indique la nécessité.
Un mot composé : 日 [zh:rì] (quotidien) + 用 [zh:yòng] (utiliser) + 品 [zh:pǐn] (articles).

segments.teaching_goal: Pour apprendre à décrire le fait d'emmener quelqu'un à un endroit et le mot pour « centre commercial ».

narration_track.subtitle_en: C'est la fin de la deuxième phrase. Elle nous dit où ils sont allés. [zh:柯林就带他们] signifie « Alors Ke Lin les a emmenés ». Ils sont allés [zh:来到], ce qui veut dire « arriver à » un endroit. Où ça ? [zh:附近一家最大的购物中心] signifie « le plus grand centre commercial des environs ». [zh:购物中心] est un terme clé pour « centre commercial ».

content.focus_text: 柯林就带他们来到附近一家最大的购物中心。
content.focus_pinyin: Kē Lín jiù dài tāmen láidào fùjìn yì jiā zuì dà de gòuwù zhōngxīn.
content.focus_gloss_en: alors Ke Lin les a emmenées au plus grand centre commercial des environs.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Le verbe pour emmener quelqu'un ou quelque chose à un endroit.
Fait référence aux environs ou à la zone environnante.
Un mot composé : 购物 [zh:gòuwù] (faire du shopping) + 中心 [zh:zhōngxīn] (centre).
Le verbe pour emmener quelqu'un ou quelque chose à un endroit.
